---
layout: default
title: "Rajya Sabha Dynamics and Judicial Reforms"
date: 2026-07-13T21:34:38.112995+00:00
summary: "BJP nears Rajya Sabha majority ahead of Monsoon Session as legal challenges and judicial transfer policies shape the national political landscape"
run_time_ist: "3:04AM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Political Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 3:04AM</p>


<h2 class="section-title">India National Politics</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>BJP Rajya Sabha majority:</strong> The BJP is nearing a historic threshold in the Rajya Sabha, potentially securing a majority for the first time since 1986 ahead of the upcoming Monsoon Session.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi-?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I1</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>NCP leadership dispute:</strong> An NCP leader has formally challenged the election of Sunetra Pawar as the party&#x27;s national president, signaling internal instability.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I2</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Women&#x27;s Reservation deadlock:</strong> The Union government faces continued scrutiny over the implementation of the Women&#x27;s Reservation Act as political hurdles delay its rollout.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I4</a></span></li>
</ul>

<h2 class="section-title">Policy and Institutions</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Judicial transfer policy:</strong> The Supreme Court Collegium, led by the Chief Justice of India, has approved a new policy to facilitate the early transfer of High Court Chief Justices.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W1</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Telangana water rights:</strong> The Supreme Court has criticized the Congress government&#x27;s handling of Telangana’s water rights, citing administrative negligence.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W1</a></span></li>
</ul>
